candidates.question: Who administers the Law?
candidates.expected_answer: Registrar
candidates.notes: Article 7 states the Law and related legislation are administered by the Registrar.

candidates.question: What body may make Regulations for the purposes of the Law?
candidates.expected_answer: Board of Directors of the DIFCA
candidates.notes: Article 58(1) identifies the rule-making body.

candidates.question: What does DIFCA stand for?
candidates.expected_answer: DIFC Authority
candidates.notes: Defined term in Schedule 1.

candidates.question: What court is established under Dubai Law?
candidates.expected_answer: DIFC Court
candidates.notes: Defined term in Schedule 1.

candidates.question: What term is used for the person appointed by decree of the Ruler pursuant to Dubai Law?
candidates.expected_answer: President
candidates.notes: Defined term in Schedule 1.

candidates.question: What is the defined term for a general partnership formed in the DIFC?
candidates.expected_answer: General Partnership
candidates.notes: Defined term in Schedule 1.

candidates.question: What is the defined term for a general partnership formed outside the DIFC and registered under Article 13?
candidates.expected_answer: Recognised Partnership
candidates.notes: Defined term in Schedule 1.

candidates.question: What is the term used for legislation made by the Board of Directors of the DIFCA for the purposes of the Law?
candidates.expected_answer: Regulations
candidates.notes: Defined term in Schedule 1.

candidates.question: Is the Law administered by the Registrar?
candidates.notes: Article 7 assigns administration to the Registrar.

candidates.question: Must a person carry on a General Partnership business in the DIFC only if the partnership is registered or recognised?
candidates.notes: Article 9 contains the registration prohibition.

candidates.question: Does each Partner have to sign the partnership agreement?
candidates.notes: Article 10 requires the agreement to be signed by all partners.

candidates.question: Can the Registrar refuse to register a General Partnership or any Partner without giving reasons and without court review?
candidates.notes: Article 12(5) states the refusal need not be reasoned and is not subject to appeal or review.

candidates.question: Unless all partners agree otherwise, can ordinary matters be decided by majority even though a change in the nature of the business requires unanimous consent?
candidates.notes: Article 17(c) distinguishes ordinary matters from changes to the business nature.

candidates.question: Does every Partner take part in management by default?
candidates.notes: Article 17(a) sets the default management rule.

candidates.question: Is a General Partnership required to appoint an auditor in every case?
candidates.notes: Article 20(7) makes an auditor optional unless the Regulations or the partnership agreement provide otherwise.

candidates.question: May a Partner use information, money, or property of the General Partnership for private purposes without agreement?
candidates.notes: Article 26 prohibits private use unless all partners agree.

candidates.question: Are Partners jointly and severally liable for debts and obligations incurred while they are Partners?
candidates.notes: Article 28(1) states the default liability rule.

candidates.question: Does an outgoing Partner remain liable for debts incurred after ceasing to be a Partner?
candidates.notes: Article 34(2) limits outgoing partner liability to pre-exit debts.

candidates.question: Does the Law require annual general meetings of the partners?
candidates.notes: Negative deterministic candidate; this meeting requirement is not stated in the document.

candidates.question: How many persons are needed at minimum to form a General Partnership?
candidates.notes: Article 8 defines a general partnership as a relationship between two or more persons.

candidates.question: For how many years must accounting records be preserved?
candidates.notes: Article 14(2)(b) and Article 18(2)(b) both require six-year preservation.

candidates.question: Within how many months after the end of the financial year must accounts be prepared and approved?
candidates.notes: Article 19(4) gives the six-month deadline.

candidates.question: Within how many days after approval must the accounts be filed with the Registrar?
candidates.notes: Article 19(5) gives the filing deadline.

candidates.question: How many days does Schedule 1 define as a week?
candidates.notes: Schedule 1 defines a week as seven days.

candidates.question: How many days does Schedule 1 define as a month?
candidates.notes: Schedule 1 defines a month as thirty days.

candidates.question: How many days does Schedule 1 define as a year?
candidates.notes: Schedule 1 defines a year as 365 days.

candidates.question: Over how many days is the United Arab Emirates Central Bank deposit-rate benchmark period stated?
candidates.notes: Article 38(c)(ii) refers to the ninety-day deposit rate.

candidates.question: What is the maximum fine in Schedule 2?
candidates.notes: Schedule 2 lists the maximum fine.

candidates.question: How many days does the Law allow for filing a partnership tax return?
candidates.notes: Negative deterministic candidate; no tax-return filing rule appears in the document.

candidates.question: Which examples are included in the definition of Document?
candidates.notes: Schedule 1 definition of Document lists the included examples.

candidates.question: Which bodies are defined as the Court and the DIFCA?
candidates.notes: Schedule 1 defines both entities.

candidates.question: Which legal categories are referenced in Schedule 1 as legislation in the DIFC?
candidates.notes: Schedule 1's legislation-in-the-DIFC section lists these references.

candidates.question: Which examples are included in the definition of Accounting Records?
candidates.notes: Schedule 1 definition of Accounting Records lists these examples.

candidates.question: What month and year is shown for the consolidated version on the cover?
candidates.expected_answer: March 2022
candidates.notes: Cover page states Consolidated Version No. 2 (March 2022).

candidates.question: What year appears in the short title citation of the Law?
candidates.notes: The short title is the General Partnership Law 2004.

candidates.question: What year is given in the definition of the Operating Law?
candidates.notes: Schedule 1 defines the Operating Law by its 2018 citation.

candidates.question: What must be included in a General Partnership registration application?
candidates.expected_answer: The General Partnership name ending with 'partnership', the registered office address in the DIFC, the nature of the business, and each partner's name and address.
candidates.notes: Article 12(2) lists the required application content.

candidates.question: What must a Recognised Partnership registration application set out?
candidates.expected_answer: Its name, its DIFC service address and authorised service person, the nature of the business, each partner's name and address, and any additional information required by the Registrar.
candidates.notes: Article 14(2) lists the required application content.

candidates.question: What do the accounting records need to do?
candidates.expected_answer: They must be sufficient to show and explain transactions, disclose the financial position with reasonable accuracy, and let the partners verify compliant accounts.
candidates.notes: Article 18(1) describes the purpose of the records.

candidates.question: What is the default management rule under Article 17?
candidates.expected_answer: Every partner manages, no new partner may join without unanimous consent, ordinary matters may be decided by majority, and the business's nature cannot change without unanimous consent.
candidates.notes: Article 17 combines three default governance rules.

candidates.question: What does Article 27 allow the General Partnership to do?
candidates.expected_answer: Indemnify any partner or other person, subject to the standards and restrictions in the partnership agreement.
candidates.notes: Article 27 grants an indemnification power.

candidates.question: What happens if a partner misapplies fiduciary money or property under Article 31?
candidates.expected_answer: The General Partnership must make good the loss.
candidates.notes: Article 31 imposes partnership liability for the misapplication.

candidates.question: What must a partner do with partnership information, money, or property for private purposes?
candidates.expected_answer: Not use it for a non-business purpose unless all partners agree.
candidates.notes: Article 26 states the private-use restriction.

candidates.question: What does Article 21 accountability cover?
candidates.expected_answer: Property, profit, or benefit derived in the conduct of the business or from using the partnership's property, name, or business connection.
candidates.notes: Article 21(1) describes the fiduciary accounting duty.

candidates.question: What is the effect of a written notice to a partner under Article 33?
candidates.expected_answer: It operates as notice to the General Partnership unless the notice is part of a fraud on the partnership by or with that partner's consent.
candidates.notes: Article 33 sets the notice rule.

candidates.question: When may a partner dissolve the partnership by written notice under Article 39A?
candidates.expected_answer: When the partnership was formed for an undefined time and no fixed adventure or undertaking, subject to Article 42 and any agreement between the partners.
candidates.notes: Article 39A(1) sets the notice-based dissolution route.

candidates.question: What must the Registrar be satisfied of before consenting to a voluntary dissolution?
candidates.expected_answer: That no outstanding liabilities, guarantees, or other legal obligations will remain after the intended dissolution.
candidates.notes: Article 42(2) states the consent condition.

candidates.question: What is the effect of dissolution on partner authority for winding up?
candidates.expected_answer: The partners' authority continues only as needed to wind up the affairs and complete unfinished transactions.
candidates.notes: Article 49 preserves authority for winding up only.

candidates.question: What must the Registrar publish for public viewing?
candidates.expected_answer: A reasonably current version of the register of current and past registrations on the DIFC website.
candidates.notes: Article 60(3) requires public web access.

candidates.question: What does the Law say about any requirement to appoint a company secretary for a General Partnership?
candidates.expected_answer: There is no information on this question in the provided documents.
candidates.notes: Negative free_text candidate; the Law does not address any company secretary requirement.
